Empires & Puzzles Costumed Victor Review: 5-Star Legendary Dark/Purple Hero
Victor traded his cape for a pinstripe suit and his single-target hit for a nasty AoE defense down. Is the 'King of Crime' the Very Fast purple support hero your roster needs? We break down the Don.
Costumed Victor Review: The Mafia Don
Costumed Victor: The Don of Very Fast Defense Down
Costumed Victor (affectionately known as "C-Vic" or "The Don") transforms the classic vampire into a
. Released as part of the Morlovia expansion, this costume fundamentally changes his role from a defensive sniper to an aggressive, multi-target support engine. With
mana, he is designed to break defenses open before the enemy even moves. He is a
, which fits his slippery, life-stealing mafia persona perfectly.

Gone is the gothic castle; enter the noir underworld. Victor dons a sharp pinstripe suit, a fedora, and carries a cane that likely conceals a blade. The skull in his hand remains (some habits die hard), but the vibe shifts from Dracula to
. It’s one of the cooler thematic shifts in the game, turning a creature of the night into a boss of the underground.
Even with the Costume Bonus, Victor's stats (approx.
without Limit Break) are considered low in the modern meta. However, thanks to the massive stats available through
, he can be pumped up to respectable levels (1200+ Atk/Def), allowing him to survive long enough to do his job. He relies on his

This skill is a fantastic opener for any purple stack.
Unlike the base version, C-Victor hits the target and nearby enemies. 155% isn't huge damage, but it softens them up.
He applies a bleed DoT to three targets. It starts at ~330 over 5 turns but increases if refreshed. This pressure adds up quickly across three heroes.
speed. This is the money maker. While -24% isn't the highest drop (compared to Tiburtus or Treevil), the
is what matters. You can fire this in 6 tiles, paving the way for your slower, harder hitters to wipe the board.
He steals 61% of healing from 3 enemies. This cripples healer-heavy defenses, turning their sustain into his sustain.

With the Costume Bonus (approx +5% mana) and the Morlovia Family Bonus (+5%/+12%), it is incredibly easy to get C-Victor to charge in 6 tiles (2 matches of 3). This requires practically no high-level mana troops if you have family members.
DoT: 330 (Base) -> up to 1225 (Max Stack) across 3 targets.
If he keeps firing, he spreads thousands of points of bleed damage while keeping defense down active.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
mana bonus is the key. Pairing him with just one other family member (like Francine or Vanda) accelerates your entire Very Fast core.

Just like the base version, C-Victor has a 65% chance to steal a dispellable buff from an enemy before hitting. Stealing a Taunt or Counterattack at Very Fast speed can completely disrupt the enemy's opening strategy.
At the start of battle, he gets an attack boost. This helps his initial hit and his DoT ticks, making him slightly more threatening right out of the gate.
When released, this costume revitalized Victor, moving him from a "has-been" to a "must-use" for rush strategies and fast offense.
In 2026, he faces stiff competition from newer heroes with higher stats and stronger effects. However, the combination of
Very Fast + Defense Down + Heal Steal
remains a potent niche. He is still a premier hero for
for offense, particularly in 3-2 or 4-1 stacks where his role is to set the table for bigger hitters. He is less effective on defense due to his low stats.

. The ability to impact three enemies with Defense Down and Healing Steal is exponentially more valuable than the single-target nature of the base form. The only reason to switch back is if you desperately need the team defense buff.

The Rogue talent (Evade) is superior for Victor because he is squishy. Dodging special skills keeps him alive to steal more health.

Softens targets for snipers like Kageburado or Seshat.

Even with costume, his base stats are dusty. He needs Limit Breaks to survive modern snipers.

Stealing buffs and firing fast makes him a menace.

Don't pair with stronger Defense Down heroes (like C-Tiburtus or Kunchen) if they fire
him, as they overwrite nicely. But if he fires
them, he will overwrite a strong Def Down (-44%) with his weak one (-24%). Be careful with firing order!
This costume turns a mediocre hero into a great one. Max him immediately. The Rogue emblems are definitely worth it for the dodge chance.
If you have modern "Monster" heroes, C-Victor might feel light on damage. However, his utility in
is unmatched. He is a safe pair of hands for your 3rd or 4th war team, guaranteeing a Defense Down option at Very Fast speed.

"Is Costumed Victor worth Summoning For? Yes, absolutely."
- The community poll reflected high praise upon release.

Costumed Victor is the mob boss who brings a gun to a knife fight—and shoots before anyone else has drawn their weapon. While he may not hit as hard as the new gods of 2026, his speed and utility make him timeless. There is always room on a roster for a guy who can break defenses and steal health in just 6 tiles.
A classic utility hero who ages gracefully thanks to his mechanics. A must-have for depth, even if he's no longer the king of the hill.
Review of Costumed Victor (King of Crime), the Very Fast Dark hero from the Morlovia family. We analyze his Hit-3 Defense Down, Bleed damage, and how he stacks up in 2026.
